marketState: neutral - rich to fair value and storage-surplus headwind offset by crowded short COT and approaching seasonal strength
summary: Front price ($3.211) sits $0.282 above fair value ($2.929, band $2.429-$3.429) and +2.3pp above its seasonal median trajectory, while storage carries a +175 Bcf surplus to the 5-year average and just built +87 Bcf last week - a bearish fundamental backdrop. Working against that, managed money is unusually net short (18th percentile of the past 5 years), a crowded position that raises short-squeeze risk, and seasonality has a mild bullish lean into September (63% odds higher in 4 weeks), though reliability is flagged Low (51pp spread). Weather demand is running about 30% above the 5-year normal but eased -7.3 in the last 24h, and critically the 1-7D/8-16D forward demand figures and the 7/14/21-day forward fair-value projections are all missing from this snapshot, which weakens confidence in any forward-looking call. Technicals are mixed: bullish only on the 5-minute chart, neutral intraday, and bearish on the daily/weekly (50<200 EMA cross, negative daily MACD). The futures curve shows a normal seasonal shape - slight front/next backwardation with a large contango buildup into Dec/Jan - consistent with typical seasonal storage dynamics rather than acute stress.

longTerm.rationale: Bullish Sep seasonality (63% odds higher in 4wks) and a crowded managed-money short (18th 5y percentile) argue for upside, but a growing storage surplus (+175 Bcf vs 5y) and price already $0.28 rich to fair value argue for downside; with 1-7D/8-16D demand and 7/14/21D fair-value forecasts all missing, there isn't enough forward-looking data to size a directional position confidently.
longTerm.invalidation: If the storage surplus narrows toward the 5y average and restored weather/fair-value forecasts confirm sustained above-normal demand into peak season, or price pulls back into the $2.90-$3.05 fair-value zone, the case for a long strengthens; a break and hold below $2.80 would instead confirm the surplus-driven downside case.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for a pullback to the $2.90-$3.05 fair-value zone with a stabilizing/narrowing storage surplus, or for 1-7D/8-16D weather and forward fair-value data to repopulate and confirm demand strength into September, before committing directionally.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price trades $0.282 above fair value while daily/weekly technicals are bearish (50<200 EMA cross, negative daily MACD) with only mild front/next backwardation, and 24h weather demand is easing (-7.3) even though still ~30% above the 5-year normal.
shortTerm.invalidation: A push and sustained hold above $3.43 (outside the fair-value band), or a bullish 1-7D extreme-heat weather surprise, would invalidate the reversion-lower thesis.

keyDrivers: Price $0.28 above fair value ($3.211 vs $2.929), still inside the $2.43-$3.43 band
Storage surplus +175 Bcf vs 5y and still building (+87 Bcf last week) - bearish fundamental
Managed money net short at 18th 5y percentile - crowded short, short-squeeze risk
Weather demand +30% vs 5y normal but cooling -7.3 day/day; 1-7D/8-16D forecasts missing
Daily/weekly technicals bearish (50<200 EMA cross), intraday timeframes mixed
risks: Missing weather forward (1-7D/8-16D) and fair-value forward (7/14/21D) data limits confidence in both trade ideas
Crowded managed-money short position raises short-squeeze risk on any bullish weather or storage surprise
Low seasonality reliability (51pp 10-90% spread) means the seasonal edge could easily fail to play out
